## Findings (2)

### [CRITICAL] Finding 1

Category:
  payments

ID:
  path-classifier.laravel.webhook-controllers

Title:
  Payments-related path changed

Message:
  File 'app/Http/Controllers/Webhooks/StripeWebhookController.php' matches path-classifier rule 'laravel.webhook-controllers' for category 'payments'.

Evidence:
  file:
    app/Http/Controllers/Webhooks/StripeWebhookController.php
  detail:
    laravel.webhook-controllers

Recommendation:
  Payment-related code is high-impact: bugs here can charge or refund the wrong amounts. Review the change against the payment-flow spec, verify with a sandbox transaction, and confirm webhook idempotency where relevant.

### [HIGH] Finding 2

Category:
  test-gap

ID:
  test-gap.laravel.webhook-controllers

Title:
  Risky change without paired test: laravel.webhook-controllers

Message:
  Changes to app/Http/Controllers/Webhooks/StripeWebhookController.php were not paired with a sibling test change in the same diff

Evidence:
  file:
    app/Http/Controllers/Webhooks/StripeWebhookController.php
  detail:
    path-rule: laravel.webhook-controllers

Recommendation:
  Add or update sibling tests for this change in the same diff. Risky-area changes without paired test changes are difficult to verify and easy to regress. If the change is intentionally test-exempt (e.g., a pure config tweak), document the exemption in the change description.

After addressing the findings, re-run `viberevert check --since baseline` to verify the report comes back clean. If your changes go wrong, recover with git or a prior checkpoint; rollback is session-scoped.
